title: fastRanges: A Practical Introduction to Genomic Interval Analysis
text: fastRanges provides high-throughput interval operations Bioconductor workflows built IRanges GRanges. genomic data, central data type GRanges. row one genomic interval : sequence name "chr1" genomic range (start, end, width) strand ("+", "-", "*") Typical analyses ask questions : peaks overlap genes promoters? many query ranges overlap subject annotation? subject nearest query? can subject annotation reused across many batches query intervals? fastRanges designed answer questions keeping grammar close Bioconductor overlap APIs IRanges GenomicRanges. package best thought high-throughput interval engine supported Bioconductor range classes rather promise full drop-replacement every findOverlaps() input type.

title: fastRanges: A Practical Introduction to Genomic Interval Analysis
text: type controls “match” means.
code: type = "any" query  :    |------| subject: |------| => any qualifying overlap is a hit  type = "within" subject: |--------------| query  :    |------| => query must lie inside subject  type = "start" query  :    |------| subject:    |------------| => start coordinates must match or be within tolerance  type = "end" query  :       |------| subject: |------------| => end coordinates must match or be within tolerance  type = "equal" query  :    |------| subject:    |------| => start and end coordinates must match, or be within tolerance

title: fastRanges: A Practical Introduction to Genomic Interval Analysis
text: max_gap controls far apart two ranges may still count hit. -1 means true overlap required 0 allows Bioconductor’s zero-gap tolerance behavior positive values allow nearby, non-overlapping ranges count modes support interpretation

title: Anti Overlap Join — fast_anti_overlap_join
text: query IRanges GRanges query object. subject IRanges/GRanges object fast_ranges_index. Use fast_build_index(subject) subject reused across many overlap queries. max_gap Integer scalar controlling far apart two ranges may still count hit. Use -1 require true overlap. Use 0 allow touching ranges "" keep Bioconductor's default tolerance behavior overlap modes. Use positive values want "nearby" ranges count matches even overlap directly. Units bases. meaning intentionally aligned IRanges::findOverlaps() / GenomicRanges::findOverlaps(). min_overlap Integer scalar minimum overlap width, bases. 0 least strict setting. Larger values require wider shared overlap therefore return fewer hits. argument matters selected type allows actual overlap width measured. type Character scalar describing "match" means. "" matches overlap satisfies max_gap / min_overlap. "start" matches ranges compatible start coordinates. "end" matches ranges compatible end coordinates. "within" matches queries contained inside subjects. "equal" matches queries subjects interval, start/end differences larger max_gap tolerance allowed. ignore_strand Logical scalar controlling strand handling genomic ranges. GRanges, FALSE means "+", "-", "*" interpreted using standard Bioconductor strand rules. TRUE means strand ignored genomic coordinates compared. IRanges, argument effect strand. threads Integer scalar number worker threads use. Use 1 conservative behavior easiest debugging. Use larger values multicore machines throughput matters. repeated-query workloads, combine prebuilt index fast_build_index(subject) thread count chosen empirically hardware. fastRanges optimized large throughput-oriented workloads. one-small jobs, Bioconductor's native overlap routines may competitive. deterministic Logical scalar controlling output order. TRUE returns stable order, useful testing, reproducible reports, direct comparison across thread counts. FALSE allows implementation return hits unspecified order, can noticeably faster large multithreaded jobs avoids extra global ordering work. query_prefix Prefix applied query columns.

title: Anti Overlap Join — fast_anti_overlap_join
text: query range set ask . subject range set compare . Core interval semantics (ASCII schematic):   middle distance gap. hit allowed distance <= max_gap (max_gap >= 0), overlap width >= min_overlap. Beginner-friendly interpretation: type = "" asks "ranges touch overlap closely enough count?" type = "start" type = "end" useful interval boundaries biologically meaningful, example transcription start end sites. type = "within" asks whether query lies inside subject interval. type = "equal" asks whether query subject describe interval, optionally endpoint tolerance max_gap >= 0. argument grammar intentionally aligned Bioconductor overlap APIs (IRanges / GenomicRanges).
